raw_text: alberto ghidini linares date of birth: february 16' 1995 address: c/ zorrilla 25, madrid mobile: +34 686130478 email: aghidini95 gmail.com summary =" aerospace engineer currently finishing an msc in aerospace engineering with an expected grade of first class with distinction, being the first in the promotion waiting to present the final thesis. = my internship related to space at crisa (an airbus defense and space company) has provided me with: - a valuable insight within the aerospace sector of work and allowed me to understand both the technical aspects as well as the analytical and investigative elements of work. - agood appreciation of how a project is developed, the interaction and tasks that are carried out by the different departments of the project. education msc aerospace engineering brunel university london, uk sept 2018 sept 2019 =" modules: advanced aerodynamics, propulsion systems, space mechanics. design and analysis of aircraft and aerospace vehicles. aircraft structures, loads and aeroelasticity. strategic management, innovation and enterprise = group project: morphing wing project focused on building and analysing a morphing wing concept under a structural point of view. = final thesis: modal analysis and optimization of an uav wing beng aerospace engineering universidad carlos iii de madrid sept 2013 jul 2018 =" modules: aerodynamics, aeroelasticity, fluid mechanics, propulsion, mechanics of flight, aircraft structures, structural dynamics propulsion, airports, onboard system design, aircraft systems = final thesis: logistics analysis of an in-situ resource utilization mission to the moon languages and it/software skills languages: spanish (native), italian (native), english (c1, ielts 2018) it/software skills: =" programming: matlab =" cad: autodesk inventor, solid edge, solidworks =" fea and cfd: abaqus, ansys, xflr5 = other: microsoft office, microsoft project, primavera, active risk manager, aaa relevant employment history project controller crisa (airbus defense and space company) feb 2018 jul 2018 successfully completed a number of assignments to provide support to the project manager of the meda project (mars environmental dynamics analyzer), which is a nasa/jpl-selected instrument for the mars 2020 mars-surface mission, consisting in the creation of environmental sensors distributed in the rover, and controlled by an electronic unit (icu), to measure wind conditions, air temperature, soil temperature, humidity, pressure, radiation and dust density. the assignments and the technical management required by the position were related to the following aspects: = control and maintenance of planning of project engineering tasks and progress in planned activities = control and maintenance of the risks identified in the project by means of the arm tool, as well as the monitoring of the mitigation actions that are defined. =" execution and registration of minutes of meeting and organization of internal and external meetings of project. technical project work modal analysis of an uav wing (msc) =" dissertation for the msc course (from june to september 2019) =" an aeroelastic study of a wing will be performed in order to analyse the natural frequencies and mode shapes, by using the software abaqus. =" optimization of the wing in terms of composite layup, material and possible design improvements of the wing will be carried out. logistics analysis of an in-situ resource utilization mission to the moon (beng) = final year dissertation (from february to july 2018) =" the scope of the project was to analyse the feasibility of fulfilling a long-term mission on the moon using exclusively local resources, with no dependency on the earth. =" the study has been carried out investigating only currently-available isru technology, with the purpose of verifying if improvements to existing technology are required to complete a mission of these characteristics. design and analysis of a cargo-aircraft (msc) = individual project in the context of design and analysis of aircraft and aerospace vehicles =" the goal of the project was to define a conceptual design of a cargo-aircraft under some given specifications, such as three different missions for three combinations of cargo and range. = the software used to develop the aircraft design was aaa (advanced aircraft analysis). = the different steps followed for the design process were: weight estimation, performance analysis, aerodynamics, overall configuration and geometry, stability and control analysis, and final optimization process morphing wing (msc) = group project where the objective was to study a possible structural/mechanism design to create a morphing wing for a specific glider and so to be able to avoid having joints for the control surfaces of the wing. =" the final design concept was a twisting wing able to generate lift. the wing was also analysed with fea and tested. interests and acquired skills interests: sports (tennis, football, golf and swimming), cultural (traveling, movies and music) acquired skills: =" the different projects carried out through the aerospace study helped me to have a good analysis of the data and also how to manage group work. = having carried out a public speaking and debate course in amherst college (usa) helped me to gain good oral and presentation skills in english. references available on request. madrid, 8t july 2019
